decisions.decision: Plan 03 followed archive parity exactly — case-sensitive 'SUBCON' literal, whole-PO flag, no backfill of existing POs
decisions.rationale: Smallest scope that closes the regression. Tolerant matching, mixed-PO attribution, and historical-data backfill are all explicit deferred-scope items documented in the SUMMARY. Closes Phase 68.1 backlog item.

decisions.decision: Cache coherency fix used in-place mutation, NOT a TTL reset
decisions.rationale: Matches existing precedent at procurement.js:1041 and 1930. TTL reset would force a full re-fetch of every MRF's sub-data on every status change — wasteful when we know exactly which PO changed. Surgical mutation in updatePOStatus is the minimal correct fix.

decisions.decision: Cross-group filter logic flipped from AND to OR (spec pivot)
decisions.rationale: Plan 02 originally implemented AND per the discuss-phase decision. User changed their mind after browser UAT — wanted both result sets to appear when one Materials and one Subcon card are active. /gsd-fast was the right vessel: 2-line change in filterPRPORecords + UAT test 4 expected text update.

decisions.decision: Bug 2 routed through /gsd-fast, NOT /gsd-debug, even though user invoked /gsd-debug for both
decisions.rationale: Bug 2 was not a defect — the code did exactly what Plan 02 specified. It was a requirements change. Surfacing this distinction to the user before consuming /gsd-debug context kept the debug session focused on the actual defect (bug 1).

next_action: Browser-verify the two follow-up fixes on http://localhost:8000 (dev server still running): (1) update a PO procurement_status via dropdown + click 🔄 — should NOT revert; (2) click Materials card + Subcon card together — should show UNION not intersection. Then flip [pending] markers in 91.2-HUMAN-UAT.md to [pass]/[fail].
